spec_match.rationale: Four patterns aligned with the TC39 proposal's motivation: search-result merge, heterogeneous user collections, lazy prime-finding with early exit, and a timestamp-sorted log merge that shows where a merge helper is needed instead of plain source-order concat.

interactive.rationale: Four pattern blocks each with a run button that exercises the pattern live and displays the output, including visible pull counts for the early-exit prime pipeline and an interleaved log timeline for the sorted-stream merge.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises search merging, heterogeneous processing, lazy early exit, and timestamp-sorted log interleaving; the page now includes each as a live pattern.

edge_cases.rationale: Pattern 3 now reports per-range pull counts, so the early-exit claim is visible (`rangeB` and `rangeC` stay untouched for the first eight primes). Pattern 4 interleaves already-sorted log streams by timestamp instead of concatenating each source wholesale. The remaining gap is the sync-vs-async pagination caveat.

references.rationale: See-also includes https://chromestatus.com/feature/5193275975794688, MDN, and the TC39 proposal.

design_system.rationale: Variables throughout, pattern-block layout uses the design system tokens, no static-card.

openQuestions.title: Add a database-pagination pattern
openQuestions.detail: Concat-ing page after page of a cursored fetch is a real-world use case that maps onto sync `Iterator.concat()` only awkwardly. Address it explicitly: either as a deliberate non-example pointing to async iterators, or as a synchronous slice-pagination case.

summary: Four honest patterns now exercise the spec and the adjacent sorted-merge use case: Pattern 3 visibly reports early-exit pull counts, and Pattern 4 interleaves timestamp-sorted logs instead of concatenating sources in blocks. Verified in Chrome Canary 151 with chrome-devtools-mcp by running search, users, primes, and log-merge controls with no console errors. The remaining follow-up is to note where async pagination is the right tool.
